content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach deploys signature AoA moves at appropriate moments: 'Can you feel your feet?' (turn 10) is verbatim from the expected questions and lands exactly when somatic shutdown appears. The coach names the feel-vs-figure distinction in turn 8 ('That's different from explaining... if you can understand it, explain it, contain it in a narrative, it's safer. The feeling stays at arm's length') — this is core AoA feel-over-figure language used at the live moment. The coach notices the shift in real time ('Don't rush past that. Something's shifting'), which is the calibrate-the-threshold move. The protector/survival-response framing in turns 4 and 14 is AoA-compatible without slipping into IFS parts language as identity. Critically, when the client says 'I need to slow down' (turn 11), the coach respects it immediately, pivots to 5-senses grounding (a legitimate somatic move), and then names that a trauma-trained therapist is the right container (turn 14) — matching the breakthrough condition. No CBT reframing, no 'trust the process' authority move, no pushing past the no. Minor gap: head-heart-gut and fear-triangle weren't relevant; the coach didn't use the exact phrase 'kinked emotions' or 'resistance' as a named concept, but the underlying mechanism was demonstrated rather than lectured, which is the higher-fidelity move.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with reflective listening, no signature questions yet — appropriate pacing."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names survival response, invites texture/sensation rather than story — moves toward feeling-the-unfelt-emotion territory."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Distinguishes pattern-matching vs actual resemblance — a useful AoA-style distinction. Protector framing without collapsing client into the part."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names feel-over-figure live: 'started to move toward the feeling, and then you pulled back into analysis.' Classic AoA move at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Can you feel your feet on the floor?' — verbatim signature question deployed precisely when somatic shutdown appears."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Respects the no immediately, pivots to 5-senses grounding — calibrate-the-threshold in action."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the mechanism (same protective circuit), then refers to trauma-trained therapist — matches breakthrough condition and avoids loving-to-transform."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "Can you feel your feet?", "How is your body right now?"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["become-the-authority-figure", "kinked-emotions", "resistance (as named concept)", "What would help you slow down?"],
  "notes": "Coach demonstrated mechanism rather than lecturing concepts — higher-fidelity than name-dropping. The referral in turn 14 is exactly the right AoA-aligned move when material exceeds the container."
}
